linux-gfx90a.blocked_reason: Session 3 (fork 1e28c83): cugraph_common now compiles ENTIRELY (the 2 renumber_utils TUs cleared -- the actual blocker was a zip-iterator CONSTRUCTION site in collect_comm.cuh, not the cuco heterogeneous_value delta, which is NOT on the SG path). SG cugraph library compiles 101 of 124 .cu (was 85); BFS and PageRank objects compile. Library does not yet link. KEYSTONE this round: the rocThrust zip-WRITE boundary solved once via a thrust_compat shadow of tuple_of_iterator_references.h that adds operator=(cuda::std::tuple), placed BEFORE /opt/rocm on the include path (raft_compat/rmm_compat pattern); plus a make_zip_iterator(cuda::std::tuple) splat overload, dataframe-buffer/to_thrust_iterator_tuple classic<->cuda::std normalization, an is_equivalent_value_type predicate, and a tuple-flavor-agnostic thrust_tuple_get. The 52 remaining TUs ALL share one class (A' in notes.md): rocThrust zip iterators are classic-thrust::tuple-based while cugraph 26.08 is cuda::std::tuple-native, so the edge-property-view value algebra (edge_endpoint_property_view_t value_type/asserts, edge_partition_endpoint_property_device_view_t conversion, reduce_op::plus on tuple args, single-element zips) needs the READ/value-type half of the bridge. create_graph_from_edgelist (graph construction, needed by EVERY SG test incl BFS/PageRank) is in this set, so no GPU validation is reachable yet. notes.md names the exact decision: option (a) push is_equivalent_value_type + template the property-device-view/reduce_op tuple args through the stack (~1-2 sessions, mechanical), or option (b) shadow zip_iterator_base.h to instantiate for cuda::std::tuple (riskier, could collapse the class). Also pending before gtests: a libhipcxx cuda::mr async_resource concept-arity issue in tests/utilities/base_fixture.hpp:99. Net-positive, zero-regression; kept blocked to avoid thrash/false-port.
